mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and expanding economy, which translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the correlation between PAYEMS growth and S&P 500 20-day returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.1) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to short-term positive S&P 500 returns as investors react to the improving economic outlook.
failure_condition: If, when RSAFS MoM growth exceeds 0.5%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is rising while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, real interest rates become negative, incentivizing investors to shift capital into equities as a hedge against inflation and in search of higher real returns, driving S&P 500 gains over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, during periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.1%) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) during a post-recession recovery signals improving labor market conditions, increased consumer confidence, and stronger aggregate demand, which collectively support corporate earnings and lead to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If, during identified post-recession recovery periods, a declining UNRATE is associated with negative average S&P 500 20-day forward returns, or if the correlation is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic deceleration, signaling reduced corporate demand and profitability, which leads to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If, when INDPRO MoM growth falls below -0.5%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects robust construction activity, strong consumer and business confidence, and overall economic expansion. This positive sentiment and real economic activity underpin corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If the rolling 3-year correlation between HOUST growth and S&P 500 20-day returns consistently falls below zero or is not statistically significant (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: An environment of extremely low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) coupled with strong real GDP growth (GDPC1) signifies highly accommodative monetary policy supporting robust economic expansion. This combination fosters strong corporate earnings and drives equity valuations higher over the medium term.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with strong growth
failure_condition: If, during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.2% and GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 2.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative on average, or the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a robust and expanding economy, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and profits. This positive economic momentum generates immediate investor optimism, leading to short-term positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If, when GDPC1 YoY growth exceeds 3.0%, the S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year rolling window.
